skill.name: api-design
skill.description: Guides REST API design with practical conventions for resources, HTTP methods, status codes, pagination, authentication, rate limits, and OpenAPI documentation.
skill.summary: Design consistent REST APIs with clear routes, responses, errors, and documentation.
skill.author: Benny9193

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and medium patterns, but review shows the skill is a Markdown-only REST API design guide. The command, reconnaissance, and weak-cryptography hits are false positives from fenced examples and API terminology; the only minor concern is a sample API key shown in a query string.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged As Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many fenced snippets as shell execution. These lines contain REST routes, JSON, status codes, headers, and OpenAPI examples, not executable Ruby or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is enclosed in Markdown code fences and consists of HTTP API examples. I found no executable code path, shell interpreter invocation, or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis labeled several lines as weak cryptography. The reviewed lines are metadata, headings, status-code documentation, checklist text, or a truncated bearer-token example, with no cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines do not implement hashing, encryption, token signing, or credential verification. The only related token text is illustrative documentation, not executable logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged GET examples and error text as reconnaissance. These are normal REST API documentation examples and do not inspect the local system or collect host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content describes HTTP requests and response messages for API consumers. There is no filesystem, process, network scanner, or host-discovery behavior.

low_findings.title: Sample API Key In Query String
low_findings.description: The authentication section includes a realistic-looking sample API key and demonstrates placing it in a query parameter while noting that this is less secure. This is a documentation concern, not secret access or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sample key appears hardcoded in documentation and is not read from the environment. The concern is clear because query-string credentials can be logged by clients, proxies, and servers.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent API design creates slow integration work and fragile clients. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical REST conventions for routes, methods, responses, errors, pagination, authentication, and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Recommends REST resource naming with plural nouns and shallow URL nesting.
Maps HTTP methods to create, read, update, and delete operations.
Defines consistent JSON response and error response structures.
Explains offset and cursor pagination patterns for collections.
Covers filtering, sorting, field selection, versioning, authentication, and rate-limit headers.
Provides a concise OpenAPI endpoint example and an API design checklist.
limitations: Focuses on REST APIs and does not cover GraphQL, gRPC, or event-driven APIs in depth.
Provides design guidance, not generated server code or runtime validation.
Uses generic examples that must be adapted to each product domain.
Mentions authentication patterns but does not provide complete security architecture guidance.

use_cases.title: Plan a New REST API
use_cases.description: Define resource names, methods, status codes, pagination, and documentation expectations before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review API Consistency
use_cases.description: Check an existing endpoint set for naming, response shape, error handling, versioning, and collection behavior.

use_cases.title: Prepare API Documentation
use_cases.description: Create endpoint documentation that includes request parameters, request bodies, response schemas, examples, and error cases.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Endpoints
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a small resource API
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-design skill to propose REST endpoints for a simple project management API with projects and tasks. Include methods, paths, and status codes.

prompt_templates.title: Standardize Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-design skill to define success, collection, and error response formats for our user account API. Include pagination metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-design skill to review these REST routes for naming, nesting, method use, filtering, sorting, and versioning. Suggest specific improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Draft OpenAPI Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-design skill to outline OpenAPI documentation requirements for a payment API. Include request schemas, response schemas, errors, rate limits, and authentication notes.

output_examples.input: Design endpoints for users and orders.
output: A resource map using plural nouns for users and orders.
Method recommendations for listing, creating, reading, updating, and deleting resources.
Status-code guidance for success, validation errors, conflicts, and missing resources.
A consistency review of data, meta, links, and error structures.
Specific naming fixes for fields and error codes.
Pagination and filtering recommendations for collection endpoints.

output_examples.input: Prepare docs for a new endpoint.
output: A documentation checklist covering description, parameters, body schema, responses, examples, and error cases.
OpenAPI-oriented guidance for request and response schemas.
Notes for authentication and rate-limit headers.

best_practices: Start with resource nouns, then map each user action to the correct HTTP method.
Define response and error formats before teams build separate endpoints.
Document pagination, filtering, sorting, authentication, and rate limits for every collection API.
anti_patterns: Do not use verbs such as getUser or createNewUser in resource paths.
Do not mix response field naming styles across endpoints.
Do not expose real API keys or recommend query-string credentials for production clients.

faq.question: What type of APIs does this skill help design?
faq.answer: It focuses on REST APIs with JSON responses, HTTP methods, status codes, pagination, authentication, and OpenAPI-style documentation.

faq.question: Can it generate production server code?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and review criteria, not runnable backend code.

faq.question: Does it support OpenAPI documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes documentation expectations and a concise OpenAPI endpoint example.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it during implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. They can use the guidance to review routes, response shapes, status codes, and endpoint documentation while editing code.

faq.question: Does it cover API security?
faq.answer: It covers authentication documentation and rate-limit headers, but it is not a complete API security checklist.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is Markdown documentation only. Static security hits were false positives, with one minor sample-key documentation concern.
